title: silene keiskei var. minor
intro: Silene keiskei var. minor, also known as the Japanese catchfly or small-flowered silene, is a flowering plant native to Japan and is a variant of the Silene keiskei species. Silene keiskei var. minor has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting digestive health, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Silene keiskei var. minor may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
